center.name: 恰帕斯州
center.summary: 恰帕斯州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联34种鸟类，包括角冠雉、小灰頭稚冠雉、眼斑吐綬雞等34种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: According to most sources the belted flycatcher is found in highlands from central Chiapas in southern Mexico south through central Guatemala and slightly into far northwestern El Salvador.{{cite book | last = | first = | title =Check-list of North American Birds | publisher =American Ornithologists' Union |edition =7th | date =1998 | location =Washington, D.C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the southeastern Mexican state of Chiapas and separately from there into western Guatemala

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the central part of the southern Mexican state of Chiapas, from southwestern Chiapas into most of Guatemala, and from extreme northern El Salvador into western Honduras

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in eastern Mexico's Sierra Madre Oriental from Tamaulipas south to Veracruz and in western Mexico's Sierra Madre del Sur from central Guerrero to Chiapas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: indigotica ranges in western Mexico from Sinaloa south to Oaxaca and Chiapas. The blue bunting inhabits dense vegetation such as scrubby areas, thickets, and the undergrowth and edges of tall forest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: oberhoseri is found from Nuevo León in northeastern Mexico south to Oaxaca, in Chiapas, and in several discrete ranges in southern Guatemala, El Salvador, and southwestern Honduras. The species has two records in Cochise County in the U.S

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This flycatcher is endemic to the Pacific lowlands of Mexico's western coast from Sinaloa to western Chiapas, while possibly extending into Guatemala, although this has not been confirmed. The total area that it lives in is estimated to be . Its range is discontinuous and it is found in low densities when present. It lives in dry deciduous forest, arid and semi-arid thorn forest, and scrubby woodland at about above sea level. This species is non-migratory

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: molybdophanes: from Chiapas in southern Mexico south into south-central Guatemala The species has also been recorded as a vagrant in southern and western Texas. The grey silky-flycatcher inhabits pine, pine-oak, and juniper forest in the subtropical and temperate zones. It also occurs in pine savanna and rarely in cloudforest. Sources differ on its overall elevational range; one states it is and another mostly and lower in winter

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The nominate grey-throated chat is found in southern Veracruz, most of Tabasco, eastern Oaxaca, and northern Chiapas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The lovely cotinga is found in southern Mexico from southern Veracruz across northern Oaxaca and Chiapas south of the Yucatán Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Nava's wren occurs in a small area of southern Mexico, in southeastern Veracruz, western Chiapas, and barely into northeastern Oaxaca

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in western and central Mexico from Sonora south to Chiapas and east in San Luis Potosí and Hidlago

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The pink-headed warbler (Cardellina versicolor) is a small passerine bird found in the southwestern highlands of Guatemala and the central and southeastern highlands of the Mexican state of Chiapas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in from Colima south through Michoacán to Guerrero and east to Oaxaca and southwestern Chiapas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: (s.) chionogaster (white-breasted hawk) occurs in highlands from far southern Mexico (Chiapas and Oaxaca), through Honduras, Guatemala and El Salvador, to Nicaragua

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: varians, Pacific slope of Chiapas, Mexico, and Guatemala and El Salvador The spot-breasted wren occurs in a wide variety of habitats including humid coastal and dry forest (intact, disturbed, and regrowing) and cocoa and citrus plantations

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: | pages =412–413 }} The subspecies has also strayed further north in Arizona and to British Colombia, California, Colorado, and Baja California. The nominate subspecies is found on the western side of Mexico from Colima south to Chiapas and occasionally occurs in western Guatemala. The thick-billed kingbird inhabits different landscapes in different parts of its range

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: chiapensis is found in Chiapas, Mexico, Guatemala, and possibly El Salvador

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: tacanensis is found in Mexico's Chiapas state

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The West Mexican chachalaca is found in a swath of southwestern Mexico between northern Jalisco and southwestern Chiapas and inland as far as southwestern Puebla

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: vanrossemi is found in Mexico from the southern parts of the states of Michoacán, México, and Puebla south to most of Chiapas

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: ovandensis is found in the southern Mexican state of Chiapas. The nominate subspecies C

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In Central America, this species occurs in the Pacific lowlands from Chiapas, Mexico to northern Nicaragua and as a separate population in Costa Rica, where its range is separated by a short distance, as a disjunct population

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: cerviniventris is found year-round from central Veracruz south through Puebla and Oaxaca to northern Chiapas. The buff-bellied hummingbird inhabits a variety of landscapes in its year-round range, most of which are semi-open to open and rather dry

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The wedge-tailed sabrewing is found in Mexico's Yucatán Peninsula and northeastern Chiapas, Belize, and northern Guatemala

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: guatemalensis, from Veracruz, Chiapas, and the Yucatán Peninsula south through Belize, Guatemala, El Salvador, Honduras, Nicaragua, and Costa Rica into western Panama The pale-billed woodpecker inhabits the interior and edges of a variety of forested landscapes

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in southern Mexico's Chiapas state and much of Guatemala

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The ocellated turkey lives only in a range in the Yucatán Peninsula in Mexico—which includes all or part of the states of Quintana Roo, Campeche, Yucatán, Tabasco, and Chiapas—as well as the northern and western parts of Belize and northern Guatemala

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The slender sheartail is found in mountains and their interior valleys from Chiapas in southern Mexico through Guatemala and northeastern El Salvador into Honduras

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: villadai is found from Oaxaca into most of Chiapas according to the IOC, Clements, and the AOS. BirdLife International extends the range of villadai into western Guatemala. The species inhabits deciduous forest, thorn forest, arid to semi-arid scrublands, gallery forest, and somewhat open landscapes with trees

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The fulvous owl is found in highland regions in the Mexican states of Chiapas and Oaxaca, Guatemala, Honduras, and El Salvador. Its existence in Oaxaca was reported in 1950, but locations from which the specimens were collected had been challenged

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The horned guan is found in the southern Mexican state of Chiapas, possibly in Oaxaca, and in southern Guatemala

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The turquoise-crowned hummingbird is found in the southern Mexican states of Guerrero, Oaxaca, and Chiapas. It inhabits a variety of landscapes including arid thorn, tropical deciduous, gallery, and secondary forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This species inhabits amidst lush vegetation, in specially moist rainforests at high elevations (). They populate trees that make up the canopy and subcanopy of the rainforest, though they can also be found in ravines and cliffs. It prefers to live in decaying trees, stumps, and abandoned woodpecker hollows. The vivid colors of the quetzal are disguised by the rainforest. The resplendent quetzal can be found from southern Mexico (southernmost Oaxaca and Chiapas) to western Panama (Chiriquí). The ranges of the two subspecies differ: P
